concept.conceptName: Procalcitonin | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge

en.name: Procalcitonin (PCT) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Procalcitonin (PCT) is the precursor peptide of calcitonin. In healthy adults, plasma PCT is below 0.05 ng/mL because production is normally restricted to thyroid C cells. In response to bacterial infection, PCT is produced by virtually all tissues and rises within 3-6 hours of the bacterial stimulus, peaking around 12-24 hours — making it earlier than CRP and more bacterial-specific [1, 2]. It is widely used in intensive care to discriminate bacterial vs viral or non-infectious inflammation, to guide antibiotic initiation, and especially to guide antibiotic discontinuation (PCT-guided stewardship reduces antibiotic exposure without increasing mortality in critically ill patients) [3]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by immunoassay (typically Brahms PCT assay). Results are reported as a mass concentration in ng/mL or µg/L (numerically identical), or less commonly as a substance concentration in pmol/L [4]. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the LOINC Hierarchy `Procalcitonin | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge`, expanded to include all its descendants. The set resolves to three LOINC "Lab Test" concepts: - *3046279 / 33959-8 — Procalcitonin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *44817130 / 75241-0 — Procalcitonin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Immunoassay (LOINC)*: method-specific variant. - *1988947 / 99099-4 — Procalcitonin [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: SI form. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`ng/mL`** (concept 8859 — nanogram per millilitre) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. `1 ng/mL = 1 µg/L` (numerically equal). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits: - **Body fluid, CSF specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a procalcitonin value: `3046279 / 33959-8 — Procalcitonin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source unit is µg/L**: numerically equivalent to ng/mL, no conversion needed. - **Source reports PCT from CSF or body fluid**: do **not** map. Out of scope. - **Do not confuse with**: calcitonin (thyroid hormone, different analyte and indication), C reactive protein (CRP, separate concept set), interleukin-6 (IL-6, separate concept set). ## References [1] Assicot M, Gendrel D, Carsin H, Raymond J, Guilbaud J, Bohuon C. High serum procalcitonin concentrations in patients with sepsis and infection. *Lancet*. 1993;341(8844):515-8. Available: https://doi.org/10.1016/0140-6736(93)90277-N [2] Meisner M. Update on procalcitonin measurements. *Ann Lab Med*. 2014;34(4):263-73. Available: https://doi.org/10.3343/alm.2014.34.4.263 [3] de Jong E, van Oers JA, Beishuizen A, Vos P, Vermeijden WJ, Haas LE, et al. Efficacy and safety of procalcitonin guidance in reducing the duration of antibiotic treatment in critically ill patients: a randomised, controlled, open-label trial. *Lancet Infect Dis*. 2016;16(7):819-27. Available: https://doi.org/10.1016/S1473-3099(16)00053-0 [4] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 33959-8 — Procalcitonin [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/33959-8/
